senses.definition: in a manner that relates to Bolshevism or the Bolshevik movement, often implying radical socialist or revolutionary ideas.
examples: The manifesto was written bolshevistically, urging immediate seizure of the means of production.
Her speech took a bolshevistically aggressive tone.

senses.definition: members or supporters of the Bolshevik party, a former political group in Russia that led the October Revolution.
examples: The Bolševists seized control of the capital during the revolution.
Old archives discuss the Bolševists and their strategies.

senses.definition: to convert a country, organization, or political movement to Bolshevism or its principles.
examples: The reformist party tried to bolshevize its platform.
They planned to bolshevize the movement through propaganda.

senses.definition: having been converted to Bolshevism or its principles; transformed in a Bolshevik manner.
examples: The organization was bolshevized after the summit.
Many laws were bolshevized to fit revolutionary ideals.

senses.definition: converts to Bolshevism or its principles; makes something align with Bolshevik ideas.
examples: The committee bolshevizes its charter each year.
Efforts to bolshevize education faced widespread opposition.

senses.definition: the act of converting to Bolshevism or shaping something to reflect Bolshevik principles.
examples: The party is bolshevizing its outreach efforts.
They spoke against bolshevizing the curriculum.

senses.definition: a British slang term meaning a difficult, unruly, or aggressive person; a troublemaker.
examples: Don't be a bolshie and wait your turn.
He acted like a bolshie during the meeting.

senses.definition: unwilling to comply; stubborn or aggressive in behavior.
examples: The bolshie team refused to back down.
Her bolshie stance surprised the coach.

senses.definition: more bolshie; more stubborn or defiant.
examples: She became bolshier after the debate.
The bolshier members stayed late to resist the changes.

senses.definition: plural of bolshie; several difficult or unruly people.
examples: The group contained several bolshies who resisted the plan.
We had to calm the bolshies before the discussion began.

senses.definition: most bolshie; the most stubborn or defiant.
examples: He is the bolshiest member of the team.
That idea was bolshiest of all.

senses.definition: stubborn or uncooperative; inclined to defiance.
examples: The bolshy student refused to follow the rules.
She gave a bolshy reply and walked away.

senses.definition: a large bag or sack, typically filled with a bulk commodity; also a surname or place name in some contexts.
examples: The farmer carried a bolson of grain.
Bolson is also a surname in various regions.

senses.definition: plural of bolson; multiple large sacks or bags, or a surname/place name in some contexts.
examples: They loaded several bolsons onto the cart.
Bolsons were stacked along the warehouse wall.

senses.definition: a long, thick pillow or cushion used for support; a structural beam, or figurative support.
examples: Place a bolster under the knee for comfort.
The system needs a bolster to support the fragile framework.

senses.definition: to give support to something; to strengthen or reinforce.
examples: They bolstered their defenses after the attack.
The data bolsters the argument for changes.

senses.definition: provided with support or reinforced; strengthened.
examples: The argument was bolstered by new evidence.
They bolstered the bridge with additional beams.

senses.definition: one who bolsters; something that provides support or reinforcement.
examples: The cushion acted as a bolsterer for the chair.
He was the bolsterer of the project team.

senses.definition: plural of bolsterer; multiple supporters or reinforcing devices.
examples: The bolstered team had several bolsterers backing it.
Manufacturers supply bolsterers for the new chairs.

senses.definition: the act of providing support or strengthening something.
examples: The team is bolstering its lineup for the season.
Bolstering public health measures helped reduce risk.

senses.definition: plural form; instances or supplies used to bolster or reinforce something.
examples: The walls received several bolsterings for safety.
They stored extra bolsterings for future use.

senses.definition: long, narrow cushions or pillows used for support.
examples: Place a bolster under your knees for comfort.
The couch has decorative bolsters along the back.

senses.definition: to provide support or reinforcement.
examples: The policy bolsters the middle class.
They bolstered the data with independent sources.

senses.definition: work or tasks that provide structural support or reinforcement, often architectural or mechanical.
examples: The contractor specified bolsterwork for the arch.
Bolsterwork was completed before the final coating.

senses.definition: a metal pin used to fasten things together; a sudden run or dash.
examples: Tighten the bolt on the chair leg.
He made a bolt for the door when he heard the alarm.

senses.definition: to run away suddenly; to fasten with a bolt.
examples: The horse bolted from the stable.
Bolt the door before you sleep.

senses.definition: the level or amount of electrical pressure or potential difference; voltage.
examples: The boltage of the battery is 12 volts.
High boltage lines power the city grid.

senses.definition: a small trumpet-like device or a term used in some contexts to denote a bolt or bolt-like component; note: infrequent or archaic.
examples: The boltant was secured into the mechanism.

senses.definition: a tool used to cut bolts or metal rods; a bolt cutter.
examples: They used a boltcutter to cut through the chain.
Keep the boltcutter sharp for clean cuts.
